title: rubus ulmifolius var. collivagus
intro: Rubus ulmifolius var. collivagus, also known as the Collivagus raspberry, is a specific variety of the bramble plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The Collivagus raspberry is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in jams and preserves, making it a versatile and valuable resource in herbal medicine.
